title: AppIO
content:
# AppIO


## Properties

- **name** (`string`): The name of the input/output used in the Application.
- **data_type** (`string`): Primitive data types are: `string`, `number`, `boolean` and `object`. If another name is used, it will be considered an object with that name. It's highly recommended using a custom name when using objects.
- **way** (`string`): The direction and characteristics of the input/output. The `cc` suffix means that in accepts or sends Control Changes on the input or output respectively.
- **storage** (`string`): The storage location of the data. `node-and-cloud` means that the data is stored in the node and in the cloud. `node` means that the data is stored only in the node. `none` means that the data is not stored.
- **unit** (`string`): The unit of the data. It's recommended to use the [UCUM](http://unitsofmeasure.org/ucum.html) standard for units.
summary: Object with properties: name, data_type, way, storage, unit

name.description: The name of the input/output used in the Application.

data_type.description: Primitive data types are: `string`, `number`, `boolean` and `object`. If another name is used, it will be considered an object with that name. It's highly recommended using a custom name when using objects.

way.description: The direction and characteristics of the input/output. The `cc` suffix means that in accepts or sends Control Changes on the input or output respectively.

storage.description: The storage location of the data. `node-and-cloud` means that the data is stored in the node and in the cloud. `node` means that the data is stored only in the node. `none` means that the data is not stored.

unit.description: The unit of the data. It's recommended to use the [UCUM](http://unitsofmeasure.org/ucum.html) standard for units.
